input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

task_context.question: Who is able to fill a larger variety of roles in the entertainment industry, Dave Moody or Ransom Riggs?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Who is able to fill a larger variety of roles in the entertainment industry, Dave Moody or Ransom Riggs?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
First-hop evidence:
- Passage 1 — Ransom Riggs: "American writer and filmmaker".
- Passage 2 — No Limit Kids: "directed by Dave Moody"; "provided the musical score".
- Passage 3 — Praise Band: "produced and directed Dave Moody".
- Passage 4 — The Moody Brothers: "Americana country music trio" (includes Dave Moody).
- Passage 5 — Dave Moody (sportscaster): "motorsports commentator" (name ambiguity).

Bridge needed:
- Confirm the complete, authoritative entertainment-industry occupations for Dave Moody (the filmmaker/musician referenced in Passages 2–4) and for Ransom Riggs.

Retrieval direction:
1. Fetch the Wikipedia page titled "Dave Moody" (and if disambiguation, also fetch "The Moody Brothers" and "Dave Moody (sportscaster)"); inspect the infobox/lead 'Occupation' and Career/Filmography/Discography sections to list all occupations (director, producer, composer, musician, etc.) for the entertainment Dave Moody.
2. Fetch the Wikipedia page "Ransom Riggs"; inspect the infobox/lead 'Occupation' and Career/Filmography sections to list all occupations.
3. Compile and compare the distinct occupations listed for each person to determine which fills a larger variety of entertainment roles.

current_output.query:
Retrieve the following Wikipedia pages and extract the requested fields:

Pages to fetch:
1. "Dave Moody" — if this is a disambiguation page, follow the link to the Dave Moody who is a musician/film director/producer and fetch that article.
2. "The Moody Brothers"
3. "Dave Moody (sportscaster)"
4. "Ransom Riggs"
5. (Optional) "No Limit Kids" (film)
6. (Optional) "Praise Band: The Movie"

For each fetched page, provide:
- Page title and URL.
- Infobox: verbatim "Occupation" field.
- Lead: the first 1–2 sentences of the article verbatim.
- Any sections titled Career / Filmography / Discography / Works / Selected credits / Filmography or similar — copy the lines that state specific roles/credits (e.g., "directed by Dave Moody", "produced by", "provided the musical score", "writer and filmmaker").
- If the page is a disambiguation page, indicate which linked entry corresponds to the entertainment Dave Moody and fetch that entry.
- A deduplicated list of all distinct entertainment occupations identified on that page (single-line comma-separated).
- If available, list years active and notable works that support the occupations (e.g., film titles, albums).

Return results grouped by person/page so I can directly compare the distinct occupations for:
- Dave Moody (entertainment/musician/filmmaker)
- Ransom Riggs

End query.

retrieved_doc_snippets: Praise Band: The Movie | Praise Band: The Movie is a 2008 faith-based film by Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures. It starred George Hamilton IV, Adam Melton and James Dana Bryan. The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was produced and directed by Dave Moody.

No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School | No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School is a 2010 direct-to-video family comedy film. It starred Bill Cobbs, Lee Meriwether, Blake Michael, Celeste Kellogg and Ashton Harrell. The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was directed by Dave Moody for Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures, who also provided the musical score for the film.

Dave Moody (sportscaster) | David Wayne "Dave" Moody is an American motorsports commentator who works for the Motor Racing Network. He is the lead turn announcer for MRN and has worked for the radio network on and off since 1983. He moved to MRN full-time in 1997 and became the lead turn announcer in 2001. Since 2003, Moody has been the host of the nationally syndicated Sirius XM show "Sirius XM Speedway" on Sirius XM NASCAR Radio.

Please follow the entry format.

source_state.summary_2: Dave Moody is credited as producer and director (Praise Band: The Movie — "produced and directed by Dave Moody") and is part of The Moody Brothers (an Americana country music trio); Ransom Riggs is described in the context as an American writer and filmmaker. Thus Dave Moody occupies a larger variety of entertainment roles (producer, director, musician) than Ransom Riggs (writer, filmmaker).

retrieved_titles: No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School
retrieved_doc_snippets: The Moody Brothers | The Moody Brothers are an Americana country music trio who gained prominence in 1985 when they were nominated for a Grammy Award for their instrumental performance of the classic fiddle tune "Cotton Eyed Joe". This trio of brothers, Carlton Moody, Dave Moody, and Trent Moody were nominated for a second Grammy in 1990 and won three International Country Music Awards in Europe.

Dave Moody (sportscaster) | David Wayne "Dave" Moody is an American motorsports commentator who works for the Motor Racing Network. He is the lead turn announcer for MRN and has worked for the radio network on and off since 1983. He moved to MRN full-time in 1997 and became the lead turn announcer in 2001. Since 2003, Moody has been the host of the nationally syndicated Sirius XM show "Sirius XM Speedway" on Sirius XM NASCAR Radio.

Praise Band: The Movie | Praise Band: The Movie is a 2008 faith-based film by Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures. It starred George Hamilton IV, Adam Melton and James Dana Bryan. The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was produced and directed by Dave Moody.

No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School | No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School is a 2010 direct-to-video family comedy film. It starred Bill Cobbs, Lee Meriwether, Blake Michael, Celeste Kellogg and Ashton Harrell. The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was directed by Dave Moody for Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures, who also provided the musical score for the film.

Dave Moody | David B. Moody is an artist, producer, songwriter and filmmaker from North Carolina. His instrumental proficiency has earned him two Grammy Award Nominations and three International CMA Awards as a member of The Moody Brothers. After becoming regular performers on the Grand Ole Opry and touring extensively throughout Europe in the 80s, the trio of Carolina brothers signed a contract with the Walt Disney Company in 1992 to open and perform their own nightly concerts at Disneyland Par ...[truncated]

Moody's Manual | Moody's Manual is a series of manuals published by the Moody's Corporation. It was first published in 1900 by John Moody, nine years before he founded Moody's. Initially called "Moody's Manual of Industrial and Miscellaneous Securities", it was later superseded by "Moody's Manual of Railroads and Corporation Securities", then by "Moody's Analyses of Investments".

Moody Memorial Library | Moody Memorial Library is one of the two central libraries at Baylor University located in Waco, Texas. Jesse H. Jones Library is connected to Moody by two hallways and serves as the second of the two central libraries at Baylor. Moody is the larger of the two central libraries.
target_state.summary_2: Dave Moody fills a larger variety of entertainment roles than Ransom Riggs: the “Dave Moody” passage lists him as an artist, producer, songwriter and filmmaker (and “Praise Band: The Movie” and “No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School” note he produced and directed and provided the musical score), while the “Ransom Riggs” passage describes him as an "American writer and filmmaker".
